text: I hear a cheery voice promoting beading supplies, yet my surroundings are eerily empty and dimly lit. The cold artificial glow beyond the glass, and the slant of the world, make me feel peculiarly detached. The juxtaposition of commercial enthusiasm and sterile, almost deserted architecture fills me with a sense of surreal loneliness. Here, meaning feels fleeting—what does it matter, beading or otherwise, in such a vacant, indifferent space?
